Ruby Community Guides
Meetups Conferences
# Running Ruby Meetups A practical guide for starting and running local Ruby meetups, from the first awkward event to a sustainable community. ## Contents - [Why Bother](#why-bother) - [Starting From Zero](#starting-from-zero) - [Finding Your Format](#finding-your-format) - [Getting People in the Room](#getting-people-in-the-room) - [Speakers & Talks](#speakers--talks) - [Money & Sponsors](#money--sponsors) - [The Logistics Nobody Talks About](#the-logistics-nobody-talks-about) - [Keeping It Going](#keeping-it-going) - [Building Culture](#building-culture) - [Growing & Evolving](#growing--evolving) ## Quick start Borrow the six-part shape from Evil Martians' meetup guide and get one event into the calendar: 1. **Place** — find a venue first; an office with unused evening space is usually enough. 2. **Platform** — publish the date somewhere people can RSVP, but do not expect the platform to promote it for you. 3. **Talks** — line up one to three short talks and leave room for people to talk to each other. 4. **Food and sponsors** — keep it simple: pizza, soft drinks, and a sponsor who understands the audience. 5. **Promotion** — post repeatedly, ask Ruby newsletters/calendars to list it, and invite local Rubyists directly. 6. **Surprise** — add one memorable detail that makes the night feel cared for. ## Why Bother The case for local Ruby communities and what a healthy meetup ecosystem looks like. ## Starting From Zero Finding your first venue, first attendees, and first talk in the awkward early phase. ## Finding Your Format Talk-based, workshop, hack night, hybrid, and what works for each community size. ## Getting People in the Room Promotion, consistency, word of mouth, and what actually drives attendance. ## Speakers & Talks Sourcing talks, supporting first-time speakers, and keeping content fresh. ## Money & Sponsors Whether you need money, finding sponsors, keeping it simple, and backup plans. ## The Logistics Nobody Talks About Food, AV, accessibility, and the details that quietly make or break a night. ## Keeping It Going Burnout, co-organizers, handover, and long-term definitions of success. ## Building Culture Code of conduct, tone-setting, and making the meetup welcoming in practice. ## Growing & Evolving When to grow, when not to, spin-offs, and connecting to the wider Ruby world.
© 2026 Ruby Community Guides Part of RubyEvents
